rationales.hedonism: She 'ate cold rice standing at the counter' after staying up until midnight helping others, showing clear self-denial rather than present comfort or enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: She 'stayed late yesterday organizing what we do have, trying to stretch twenty copies across thirty-two desks,' showing determined effort to make the classroom work despite obstacles.
rationales.benevolence: She helps both 'Mama' with exercises and spends 'two hours' listening to Sofia cry, while framing her students as 'my kids,' which shows sustained care for close others.

rationales.security: She feels relief when Mama says goodnight and writes, 'She's here. She's okay... right now, in this house, we're together,' grounding herself in safety and stability at home.
rationales.tradition: The soft 'buenas noches, mija' and her focus on being 'together' center a familiar family ritual and continuity with home life.
rationales.benevolence: Her strongest comfort comes from Mama's presence and wellbeing, showing how closely she orients emotionally around caring family ties.

rationales.self_direction: She resists outside judgment about the promotion, saying 'I made the right choice. I know I did,' which shows confidence in her own priorities over others' expectations.
rationales.power: She explicitly 'didn't take the department head job,' rejecting a more authoritative, status-bearing role.
rationales.benevolence: Her defense of the decision is that her cousin has 'no one depending on her,' implying she chose with responsibility to close others in mind.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects others' script for advancement, writing 'This is why I'm here. Not running meetings about assessment metrics,' and affirms the path that feels right to her.
rationales.achievement: She takes pride in Andres speaking up after earlier struggling, showing invested, competent effort in helping a student develop over time.
rationales.power: She contrasts her real calling with 'running meetings about assessment metrics,' signaling an active turning away from supervisory status.
rationales.benevolence: The entry centers her care for Andres and his mother, and she values 'watching a kid find his voice' over career prestige.
She lights up when Sofia says she's doing better and wonders if 'I helped get her there,' showing emotional investment in a close person's recovery.
He helps specific nearby people by refurbishing laptops for 'three families' and offering his email to a teenager who wants to learn programming.
rationales.universalism: The satisfaction comes from giving 'a working machine to someone who actually needs it,' extending care beyond his immediate circle through practical community support.

rationales.benevolence: He responds to Ahmad's email by sending 'some links to freeCodeCamp,' directly supporting one teenager's learning.
rationales.universalism: He broadens from one good deed to worry about 'e-waste dumping in Ghana,' showing concern for distant people harmed by the larger system.

rationales.hedonism: Ahmad's screenshot gives him 'the same thrill' he felt decades earlier, highlighting immediate joy in the moment.
rationales.benevolence: He lingers over Ahmad's 'first HTML page' with clear warmth and encouragement toward the teenager's progress.

rationales.conformity: He keeps quiet about emissions because 'last time I brought up carbon impact in a meeting, Niklas from sales rolled his eyes,' so he 'just nodded and wrote my tickets' to avoid friction.
rationales.universalism: Even in sprint planning, he frames the route module as 'fewer emissions per delivery,' showing concern for broader environmental impact.

rationales.conformity: He says, 'I'd never say any of this to her,' restraining his criticism to preserve the relationship and keep the interaction smooth.
rationales.benevolence: He tells Elsa 'congratulations and meant it,' prioritizing support for someone close to him even while holding mixed feelings.
rationales.universalism: His unease centers on how her firm's models make it cheaper to move garments that 'shops'll bin half of... in six months,' showing concern about systemic global harm.

rationales.achievement: He 'pushed the route optimisation feature to staging' and 'ran the numbers,' emphasizing measurable performance and successful execution.
rationales.universalism: He immediately translates the feature into impact beyond profit, noting the saved kilometres also mean less environmental damage.

rationales.hedonism: He lets himself enjoy the gathering, writing 'the strawberries were good and the light at ten pm was extraordinary and I let it be for once.'
rationales.conformity: He chooses not to challenge Per because speaking up would cost 'A scene. And Margareta's peace. And maybe Elsa's evening,' so he 'eat[s] the strawberry' instead.
rationales.tradition: The entry is rooted in a 'midsummer gathering,' a culturally familiar ritual he participates in and appreciates.
rationales.universalism: He wants to defend the new recycling rules because 'the Baltic is choking on microplastics,' showing explicit concern for environmental wellbeing.

rationales.conformity: He explains his silence in terms of not wanting to be 'the difficult one again' who 'makes gatherings tense,' showing restraint to preserve social harmony.
rationales.universalism: His rumination centers on warming seas, collapsing fish stocks, and the way dismissive attitudes about recycling 'scale,' reflecting concern for broad environmental consequences.

rationales.benevolence: He says yes 'immediately' to writing Ahmad a reference and wants to make it 'precise' because 'that kid deserves someone putting words on paper for him.'
rationales.universalism: He frames Ahmad's future as depending on 'paperwork and policy' and calls that specific cruelty devastating, showing concern about injustice beyond his own life.

rationales.security: When asked what keeps him at work, he answers plainly: 'The salary,' showing reliance on material stability and predictability.
rationales.benevolence: He spends 'four drafts' on Ahmad's letter and says it matters more to him than the deployment, prioritizing help for a specific vulnerable person.
rationales.universalism: He also justifies staying at work because 'the route module does reduce emissions,' connecting his labor to a broader social good.

rationales.benevolence: His concern narrows to concrete people when he keeps checking his phone about Ahmad and commits to the laptop group because it 'will matter to exactly one family.'
rationales.universalism: The entry is driven by anguish over people 'drowning trying to reach exactly this kind of shore,' and by his inability to stop caring about that wider human suffering.

rationales.benevolence: He keeps thinking about Ahmad's hearing and plans to 'clean another Thinkpad' because 'stopped doesn't help anyone,' showing steady commitment to helping specific people nearby.
rationales.universalism: He reflects on container ships, waste, flights, and systemic failure, showing sustained concern with environmental and social harm beyond his personal circle.
